message:
chore: close bd-1i9md (custom message parity), refactor doom-style test helper

Close bd-1i9md after custom message parity fix validation. Extract
create_doom_like_package() helper from the doom-style extension test
for reuse.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1i5x","title":"Implement pi.sendMessage() and pi.sendUserMessage() for message injection","description":"Allow extensions to inject messages into the conversation.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
// Custom message (not for LLM, optional display)
pi.sendMessage({
  customType: string,
  content: string,
  display?: boolean,
  details?: unknown
}, {
  triggerTurn?: boolean,
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp' | 'nextTurn'
});

// User message (triggers LLM turn)
pi.sendUserMessage(content, {
  deliverAs?: 'steer' | 'followUp'
});
```

## Delivery Modes
- **steer**: Interrupt after current tool, skip remaining tool calls
- **followUp**: Queue after agent finishes current turn
- **nextTurn**: Default, processed as next user input

## Implementation Requirements
1. Hostcall handlers for 'sendMessage' and 'sendUserMessage'
2. Integration with agent message queues (steering_queue, follow_up_queue)
3. Custom message type in session entries
4. Display logic in TUI for custom messages

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.sendMessage({customType: 'bookmark', content: 'Saved\!'})
2. Hostcall creates CustomMessage entry
3. If display=true, shows in TUI
4. If triggerTurn=true, queues for processing

## Test Plan
- Unit test: sendMessage creates correct entry type
- Unit test: sendUserMessage triggers agent turn
- Integration test: steer mode interrupts current processing
- Integration test: followUp mode queues correctly

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (hostcall handlers)
- src/extensions_js.rs (bridge to Rust)
- src/agent.rs (message queue integration)
- src/interactive.rs (display custom messages)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:10:41.477300174Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:06:55.777896986Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:06:55.777832025Z","close_reason":"pi.sendMessage() and pi.sendUserMessage() both fully implemented as hostcalls in src/extensions.rs (line ~5531 and ~5606). ExtensionHostActions trait defines both methods. Hostcall handles validation, permission checks, and message delivery.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1i5x","depends_on_id":"bd-2ca4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:00Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1i5x","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1i9md","title":"Interactive ExtensionSession get_messages omits custom messages","description":"InteractiveExtensionSession::get_messages filters out SessionMessage::Custom even though SessionHandle includes custom messages and the nearby spec comment implies custom messages belong in the extension-visible message set. This causes interactive extensions to see a different message history shape than non-interactive sessions.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-08T18:43:20.469622978Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T18:43:20.469622978Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

This causes interactive extensions to see a different message history shape than non-interactive sessions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-08T18:43:20.469622978Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-08T22:23:26.560903453Z","closed_at":"2026-03-08T22:23:26.560880340Z","close_reason":"Custom message parity fix landed and support validation passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

- Consider both cold-start and warmed caches.
- Document measurement methodology and hardware class.

Acceptance:
- Budgets are explicit and agreed, with rationale.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:25:38.338785747Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:27.719300242Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T09:51:04.846233627Z","close_reason":"Completed: add explicit extension runtime perf budgets + methodology","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1ilq","title":"Implement pi.registerShortcut() for extension keyboard bindings","description":"Allow extensions to register custom keyboard shortcuts.

## API Spec (from legacy)
```typescript
pi.registerShortcut(key, {
  description: string,
  handler: (ctx) => Promise<void>
});
```

Key format: 'ctrl+u', 'alt+shift+x', 'f1', etc.

## Implementation Requirements
1. ShortcutRegistry in extension runtime
2. Hostcall handler for 'registerShortcut'
3. Integration with keybindings system (src/keybindings.rs)
4. Validation that key isn't reserved (interrupt, clear, exit, suspend)
5. Shortcut execution via extension runtime

## Reserved Keys (cannot be overridden)
- ctrl+c (interrupt)
- ctrl+d (exit)
- ctrl+l (clear)
- ctrl+z (suspend)

## Data Flow
1. Extension calls pi.registerShortcut('ctrl+u', {...})
2. Hostcall validates key not reserved, stores in registry
3. KeyMsg in bubbletea checked against extension shortcuts
4. If match, invoke JS handler with ExtensionContext

## Test Plan
- Unit test: registerShortcut stores shortcut
- Unit test: reserved keys rejected
- Integration test: registered shortcut triggers handler

## Files to Modify
- src/extensions.rs (ShortcutRegistry)
- src/extensions_js.rs (hostcall handler)
- src/keybindings.rs (integrate with extension shortcuts)
- src/interactive.rs (dispatch to extension handler on key)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:09:48.738747397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T04:37:50.000036938Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T04:37:49.999974472Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ilq","depends_on_id":"bd-2bnc","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ilq","depends_on_id":"bd-37qz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1imi","title":"Publish charmed-harmonica crate (crates.io readiness + workflow)","description":"# Scope

# Acceptance
- Dry-run publish succeeds.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:28:50.907623666Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T00:53:55.495210323Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T00:53:55.495132268Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["charmed_rust","crates"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1imi","depends_on_id":"bd-cccv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:09Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":3338,"issue_id":"bd-1imi","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Completed `charmed-harmonica` publish-readiness validation in `../charmed_rust`.

Evidence

- `cargo publish -p charmed-harmonica --dry-run --locked` ✅
- Workspace release workflow is tag-gated + token-gated for publish (`.github/workflows/release.yml`).

Dry-run succeeded; crates.io existing-version warning is expected/non-blocking.","created_at":"2026-02-06T00:53:52Z"}]}
